text: The surgeon uses a flexible endoscope with a distal cap attachment and an insulated tip needle knife to perform a septotomy on a Zenker's diverticulum. The mucosal and submucosal layers are incised stepwise, revealing the cricopharyngeal bar. The endoscopic myotomy continues down to the base of the Zenker's pouch, allowing the endoscope to pass into the proximal esophagus.

The surgeon observes a mucosal defect with mild bleeding following the myotomy. A Conmet dura clip is applied to close the septotomy defect in the upper esophagus. The clip, with an 11mm jaw and small tail, is rotated and deployed with tactile feedback, effectively closing the defect. A second clip is placed to address a residual defect, successfully closing the septotomy.
